references.raw: [64] R. Michael, et al., —Tokenlearner: What can 8 learned tokens do for images and videos?.— arXiv preprint arXiv:2106.11297, 2021, doi: 10.48550/arXiv.2106.11297. Algorithm 1 Summary of procedure for the proposed method 1: Input: Input HSI data X ∈RH×W×C0, ground truth L ∈RH×W; patch size p; fixed training samples. 2: Output: Predicted labels of the testing samples. 3: Initialization: Optimizer: AdamW; Learning rate: 0.0005; Criterion: CrossEntropy; Epochs: 300; Batch size: 64. 4: Procedure: 5: Apply principal component analysis (PCA) on X ∈RH×W×C for dimension reduction, getting C1. 6: Crop patch X(i, j) centered at pixel x(i, j) from samples. 7: Perform depth-wise convolution on X(i, j) from feature dimension C1 to C2, generating local features. 8: Perform point-wise convolution on X(i, j) then, serving as dynamic posi- tional embedding. 9: MiM model: 10: Apply four types of MCS on X(i, j), getting sequences, S(i, j) , S(i, j) , S(i, j) , and S(i, j) (Eqs. 14a-19a). 11: for m = 1 to 4 in S(i, j) m do 12: Split S(i, j) m into sub-sequences, sf and sb (Eq. 21). 13: T-Mamba encoder: 14: for Both forward pass sf and backward pass sb do 15: Feed sub-sequences sf, sb into respective Conv1d and S6 model, ob- taining esf and esb (Eqs. 22a-23b). 16: Apply GDMs to esf and esb, resulting in bsf and bsb (Eqs. 24a and 24b). 17: Merge two features bsf and bsb, getting bs (Eq. 25). 18: Deploy STL on bs to generate down-sampled semantic tokens u (Eq. 26). 19: Apply STF on u and original feature z to generate enhanced tokens bu (Eq. 27). 20: Apply a residual connection between bu and pooled S(i, j) m , producing current output o(i, j) m (Eq. 28). 21: Activate o(i, j) m using the Tanh function. 22: end for 23: end for 24: Merge four outputs, o(i, j) , o(i, j) , o(i, j) , and o(i, j) , by WMF for weighted fusion, recorded as current scale (p) feature O(i, j) p (Eq. 49). 25: Iterate MiM models on o(i, j) , o(i, j) , o(i, j) , and o(i, j) , until the patch size p is reduced to 1 (Eq. 52). 26: Train the model using a decoder (MLP) and a multi-scale loss function (Eq. 53).
